mtd_deadline.text:
Only {days} days until Making Tax Digital goes live for sole traders.

If you haven't started tracking your income and expenses quarterly yet, now is the time.

Don't leave it until the last minute.

{days} days.

That's how long UK sole traders have before MTD changes everything.

Quarterly submissions to HMRC become mandatory from April 6.

Are you ready?

April 6 is coming fast.

From that date, 4.2 million UK sole traders must submit quarterly updates to HMRC under Making Tax Digital.

Here's what you need to know (thread):

The MTD deadline is {days} days away.

Most sole traders I've spoken to don't know what MTD requires.

Here's the simple version:
- Track income & expenses digitally
- Submit quarterly to HMRC
- Use compatible software

That's it. Start today.

Countdown: {days} days to MTD.

Still using spreadsheets? They won't be enough after April 6.

HMRC wants digital records submitted quarterly.

Time to get organised.

mtd_educational.text:
MTD explained simply:

1. Track all business income
2. Categorise expenses into HMRC's 16 categories
3. Submit a quarterly update
4. File an annual declaration

It's not complicated. It just needs a system.

HMRC has 16 expense categories for sole traders.

Do you know all of them?

Most people only use 5-6.

You could be missing legitimate deductions that save you tax.

Common MTD myth: "I need expensive accounting software."

Reality: You need digital records and a way to submit quarterly. There are affordable options.

Don't overpay for compliance.

The 4 quarterly deadlines under MTD:

Q1: April 6 - July 5
Q2: July 6 - October 5
Q3: October 6 - January 5
Q4: January 6 - April 5

Mark your calendar. Missing these will cost you.

What actually counts as a business expense under MTD?

- Office costs & supplies
- Travel & vehicle costs
- Clothing (work-specific only)
- Staff costs
- Financial costs
- Marketing & advertising

Track everything. Categorise as you go.

MTD doesn't change HOW MUCH tax you pay.

It changes HOW you report it.

Quarterly instead of annually. Digital instead of paper.

Same tax. Better system. Less last-minute panic.

If you're a UK sole trader earning over the tax threshold, MTD applies to you from April 6.

This isn't optional.

The good news: getting organised now means no stress later.

freelancer_tips.text:
3 things I wish I knew before going freelance in the UK:

1. Set aside 25-30% of every invoice for tax
2. Track expenses from day one
3. Your first year's tax bill includes payments on account

Learn from others' mistakes.

The biggest mistake new freelancers make:

Not separating business and personal expenses.

Get a separate account. Track everything. Future you will be grateful.

Freelancer tip: Don't wait until January to sort your tax.

Spend 15 minutes every week categorising expenses.

It takes 15 minutes weekly or 15 hours in January.

Your choice.

UK freelancers: you can claim these expenses most people forget:

- Home office proportion of bills
- Phone & internet (business %)
- Professional subscriptions
- Training courses
- Bank charges & fees

Every pound counts.

The freelancer's quarterly checklist:

- Reconcile all income
- Categorise every expense
- Check tax reserve is on track
- Review upcoming deadlines
- Back up your records

15 minutes. Done.

Your accounting doesn't need to be complicated.

But it does need to be consistent.

Pick a system. Use it every week. That's the entire secret to stress-free tax.

productivity.text:
Automate the boring stuff.

Your time is better spent on the work that matters, not on admin and paperwork.

Every hour you spend on manual tracking is an hour you're not earning.

The best tools don't add complexity.

They remove it.

If a tool makes your life harder, it's the wrong tool.

Simple systems beat complex ones.

Every time.

The best productivity system is the one you actually use.

Three things every freelancer should automate:

1. Expense tracking
2. Invoice follow-ups
3. Tax deadline reminders

Stop doing these manually. You have better things to do.

You didn't become a freelancer to spend hours on admin.

You did it for freedom.

Smart tools give you that freedom back.

stats_and_facts.text:
4.2 million UK sole traders.

All affected by MTD from April 2026.

Most aren't prepared.

Are you?

Average cost of an accountant for quarterly MTD submissions: over 100 per filing.

That's 400+ per year just for compliance.

There has to be a better way.

68% of sole traders say they feel unprepared for MTD.

If that's you, you're not alone.

But April 6 doesn't care if you're ready or not.

Start today.

The UK has more sole traders than any other business structure.

4.2 million.

Yet most compliance tools are built for companies with 50+ employees.

That needs to change.
